cards.noteRaw: Project level value - if one were to be using shot reference for either using directly or using to drive generations.
cards.title: Performance-first

cards.noteRaw: Project level value - if one were planning to generate the film using SCF
cards.title: Generation-first

cards.noteRaw: might contain a LoRA for example
cards.title: visual_indentity

cards.noteRaw: might contain an ElevenLabs voice
cards.title: voice_indentity

cards.noteRaw: might contain mocap FBX
cards.title: motion_behavior

cards.noteRaw: A character is not a specific visual because it could be the character as a child or as an elderly person, before the accident and after etc.
cards.title: Conditional Binding

cards.noteRaw: when present, becomes Canonical, superseding the need for for other designed reference.
cards.title: Performance Corpus

cards.noteRaw: One can define the intended workflow at the top of the project which defines the preferences, but exceptions can explicitly made. A performance ref for a generation-first project and vice versa.
cards.title: Workflow declared, exceptions explicit.

cards.noteRaw: Workflows should be non-destructive (within the format). Sources stay intact and modifications are a layer (such as a crop or a frame extraction from an image sequence).
cards.title: Preserve information (inputs), never destroy

cards.noteRaw: This is largely for the character bundle pattern but it should be generalized enough to allow other entities to use it later in the design process.
cards.title: Universal Bundle Pattern

cards.noteRaw: This is not a format production tracking, this is for narrative element collection only. Upstream tools like Ftrack or OMC
cards.title: Story First Approach

cards.noteRaw: slimmed down from current implementation, aspects are split out into the tier 2 description entities.
cards.title: character

cards.noteRaw: already exists in the schema for a variety of uses.
cards.title: character_variant

cards.noteRaw:
physical_character_profile, 
vocal_profile, delivery_profile, 
facial_expression_profile, 
character_appearance_profile, costume, 
costume_progression, 
makeup_hair_design, 
character_color_identity, 
physical_habit, 
character_relationship
cards.title: Tier 2 Entities

cards.noteRaw: named, intent-typed collection of assets (versionable)
cards.title: bundle

cards.noteRaw: junction linking assets into a bundle with role and order
cards.title: bundle_asset

cards.noteRaw: applies a bundle to a character under specific conditions
cards.title: character_asset_binding

cards.noteRaw: known-good single-frame canonical references for QA and locking
cards.title: indentity_anchor

cards.noteRaw: project-level singleton describing the body of capture
cards.title: performance_corpus

cards.noteRaw: project-level minimal entity, seperate from character because mukiple actors can play the same character (young, old etc.)
cards.title: actor

cards.noteRaw: junction (actors and characters with role context)
cards.title: actor_character_role

cards.noteRaw: a single recorded take
cards.title: take

cards.noteRaw: junction (takes can cross scenes)
cards.title: take_scene

cards.noteRaw: a meaningful within-scene segment of a take
cards.title: clip

cards.noteRaw: junction (which characters appear in which clips)
cards.title: clip_character

attributes.description: a bundle might contain sub-bundles declaring intent for the related content.
What the character is, narratively and personally. No media, no presentation specifics.
Tool-agnostic mechanism for attaching media to characters under conditions.
Project-level index of captured footage. Only populated when shooting happens, but always queryable.
Tool-agnostic mechanism for attaching media to characters under conditions.
